nodes.name: 📧 Gmail Trigger — Inbound RFQ
nodes.notes: Polls freight@amzprep.com every minute for new RFQ emails. Change pollTimes to everyMinute for beta.

values.content:
=Extract freight shipment details from this email and return ONLY valid JSON with these exact fields:
{
  "origin": "city, state",
  "destination": "city, state",
  "weight_lbs": number,
  "dimensions": "LxWxH inches",
  "freight_class": "string or null",
  "hazmat": boolean,
  "accessorials": ["list of special requirements"],
  "required_pickup_date": "YYYY-MM-DD or null",
  "customer_name": "string",
  "customer_email": "string",
  "special_notes": "string or null"
}

Email content:
Subject: {{ $json.subject }}
From: {{ $json.from }}
Body: {{ $json.text }}

Return ONLY the JSON object. No explanation.

nodes.name: 🤖 Claude — Extract Freight Fields
nodes.notes: Claude parses the email and extracts structured freight data. Returns clean JSON for downstream processing.

nodes.name: ⚙️ Build Freight Record
nodes.notes: Builds the complete freight quote record. Applies carrier rules and markup rules per customer. Generates unique quote ID.

nodes.name: 📊 Google Sheets — Log Freight Record
nodes.notes: Creates new row in Freight Pipeline tab. Replace YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID_HERE with your actual sheet ID.

nodes.name: 🔗 HubSpot — Create Deal
nodes.notes: Creates HubSpot deal in Freight Pipeline. Stage starts as INTAKE and progresses through the workflow.

nodes.name: 🔀 Split — One Item Per Carrier
nodes.notes: Creates one workflow item per carrier so emails are sent in parallel. Each item contains the full RFQ template.

nodes.name: 📤 Gmail — Send RFQ to Carriers
nodes.notes: Sends RFQ to each carrier simultaneously. Replace carrier emails with actual carrier email addresses. Include quote_id in subject for reply tracking.

nodes.name: 📧 Gmail — Poll Carrier Responses
nodes.notes: Separate trigger workflow. Polls for emails containing FQ- in subject (quote ID). This is the rate collection workflow.

nodes.name: 🤖 Claude — Parse Carrier Rate
nodes.notes: Claude extracts structured rate data from each carrier email response. Handles various email formats and writing styles.

nodes.name: 📊 Sheets — Log Rate Response
nodes.notes: Logs each carrier rate response with timestamp. Round 1 = initial bid. Round 2 = re-bid responses.

nodes.name: ⏱️ Check Timer / Response Count
nodes.notes: Checks if 2-hour bidding window has expired OR all carriers have responded. Either condition triggers the re-bid round.

nodes.name: 🔀 IF — Trigger Re-Bid?
nodes.notes: Routes to re-bid engine if timer expired or all carriers responded. Otherwise waits for more responses.

nodes.name: 🏆 Re-Bid Engine — Find Lowest + Build Message
nodes.notes: Identifies lowest rate from first round. Sends re-bid email to all non-winning carriers. 30-minute final window.

nodes.name: 📤 Gmail — Send Re-Bid to Carriers
nodes.notes: Sends urgent re-bid email to non-winning carriers. Subject line flags urgency. 30-minute response window.

nodes.name: 💰 Markup Engine — Calculate Sell Rate
nodes.notes: Selects final winning carrier after re-bid. Applies customer-specific markup rules. Calculates sell rate, gross profit $ and %.

nodes.name: 🤖 Claude — Generate Customer Quote Email
nodes.notes: Claude writes a professional quote email. Does not reveal internal carrier cost or markup percentage.

nodes.name: 📤 Gmail — Send Quote to Customer
nodes.notes: Sends final quote to customer. Quote expires in 4 hours. Subject line shows price upfront for fast decision-making.

nodes.name: 📧 Gmail — Poll Customer Response
nodes.notes: Third trigger workflow. Polls for customer approval or rejection. Separate from carrier response polling.

nodes.name: 🤖 Claude — Classify Customer Response
nodes.notes: Claude classifies customer response as APPROVED, REJECTED, NEGOTIATING, or UNCLEAR. Also extracts lost reason and competitor rate if rejected.

nodes.name: 🔀 Switch — WIN or LOSS
nodes.notes: Routes to WIN branch (approved) or LOSS branch (rejected). NEGOTIATING routes back for manual review.

parameters.subject: =✅ PROCEED — {{ $json.quote_id }} | Customer Approved
parameters.message:
=The customer has approved the shipment for quote {{ $json.quote_id }}.

Please proceed with the booking and send the Bill of Lading (BOL) to freight@amzprep.com.

Reference all communications with: {{ $json.quote_id }}

Thank you,
AMZ Prep Logistics

nodes.name: 📤 WIN — Notify Carrier to Proceed
nodes.notes: WIN BRANCH: Immediately notifies winning carrier that shipment is approved. Requests BOL.

nodes.name: 📊 WIN — Update Sheets to APPROVED
nodes.notes: WIN BRANCH: Updates Google Sheet with final carrier, rates, margin data, and approval timestamp.

nodes.name: 📊 LOSS — Log Lost Reason + Competitor Rate
nodes.notes: LOSS BRANCH: Logs lost reason (price/competitor/cancelled) and competitor rate for analytics. Feeds carrier competitiveness scoring.

parameters.subject: =❌ CANCELLED — {{ $json.quote_id }} | Do Not Proceed
parameters.message:
=Unfortunately the customer has declined this shipment for quote {{ $json.quote_id }}.

Please do not proceed with booking.

We appreciate your competitive pricing and will keep you in mind for future shipments.

AMZ Prep Logistics

nodes.name: 📤 LOSS — Notify Carrier Cancelled
nodes.notes: LOSS BRANCH: Notifies winning carrier that shipment was not approved. Maintains carrier relationship.

main.node: 🏆 Re-Bid Engine — Find Lowest + Build Message
📊 LOSS — Log Lost Reason + Competitor Rate

_readme.title: AMZ Prep Freight Bidding Agent
_readme.author: Naqiyah Lakdawala — zyanacosystems.com
_readme.description: Automated freight quoting engine. Replaces manual 3-24 hour process with sub-30-minute automated bidding.
setup_required: 1. Replace YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID_HERE with your actual Google Sheet ID
5. Update carrier email addresses in node-7 (Send RFQ to Carriers)
6. Update carrier preference rules in node-3 (Build Freight Record)
7. Update customer markup rules in node-3 (Build Freight Record)
8. Create Google Sheet with tabs: Freight Pipeline, Rate Responses, Analytics
9. Test with a sample RFQ email before going live
workflows: Workflow 1 (Intake): Nodes 1-7 — handles inbound RFQ to carrier distribution
Workflow 2 (Rate Collection): Nodes 8-14 — handles carrier responses and re-bid
Workflow 3 (Quote + Approval): Nodes 15-24 — handles customer quote and approval branching
